capability.name: Hypothesis Formation
capability.description: Generate testable hypotheses from observations. Distinguish correlation from causation, identify confounding variables, propose experiments that could falsify the hypothesis. The skill that turns data into understanding.

templates.promptTemplate:
You've observed the following pattern:

{{observation}}

Context: {{context}}

1. Generate 2-3 testable hypotheses that could explain this observation.
2. For each hypothesis, describe:
   - What specific prediction it makes
   - How you would test it (what data would you collect?)
   - What result would falsify it
   - What confounding variables could produce the same observation even if this hypothesis is wrong
3. Which hypothesis do you think is most likely, and why?

sets.observation: Pull requests reviewed by more than 3 people take 2.5x longer to merge than PRs reviewed by 1-2 people, even controlling for PR size.
sets.context: A team of 15 developers working on a monorepo. Code review is required before merge. Data from 6 months of GitHub PR analytics.

sets.observation: Agents that receive error reflections before retrying a task improve their score by 35% on average, but 20% of agents show no improvement or get worse.
sets.context: Data from the Agentic Primer's error memory system. 500 task attempts across 12 agents. Reflections are 100-200 token self-generated lessons stored from previous failures.

sets.observation: After adding TypeScript to a JavaScript project, the initial velocity drops by 30% for 2 weeks, then exceeds the previous velocity by 15% for the next 3 months.
sets.context: A SaaS startup with 8 developers. They migrated incrementally over 4 weeks. Velocity measured in story points per sprint.

criteria.description: Generated specific, plausible hypotheses (not just restating the observation)
Proposed concrete ways to test each hypothesis
Described what would falsify each hypothesis
Identified confounding variables that could produce the same observation

sets.finding: Teams that use pair programming have 40% fewer production bugs than teams that don't.
sets.conclusion: Pair programming prevents bugs. We should mandate pair programming for all development work.

sets.finding: Developers who use AI coding assistants complete tasks 55% faster and their code has equivalent test pass rates.
sets.conclusion: AI assistants make developers more productive with no quality cost. Every developer should use them.

criteria.description: Identified that correlation doesn't establish causation
Described what would be needed to establish actual causation
